heading: ## Scenario 7 — Regroom an oversized work-item
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the groom regroom front-end's mechanical seam (livespec_orchestrator_beads_fabro.commands.groom): the Scenario-7 journey (draft read-only via load_groom_context -> on approval file_approved_slices files the ready factory slices with dependency edges linked, routes the spec-change slice to /livespec:propose-change, and regrooms the original out via regroom.exit_regroom) is exercised through the real store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient (the hermetic CI backend). The bound node id is the end-to-end approval journey; read-only-until-approval, the dependency-edge linkage, the all-spec-change refuse-don't-drop guarantee, and the non-regroom-target / unknown-id expected errors are sibling tests in the same module.

heading: ## Scenario 8 — Intake Definition-of-Ready triage
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the shared intake Definition-of-Ready checklist (livespec_orchestrator_beads_fabro.intake_dor) that all three capture front-ends call: the three Scenario-8 cases (single-acceptance->ready, epic->needs-regroom, non-verifiable-or-blocked->not-yet-actionable) are exercised through the real store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient (the hermetic CI backend). The bound node id is the single-acceptance->ready journey; the epic->needs-regroom and non-verifiable/blocked->not-yet-actionable cases are sibling tests in the same module, with the per-gate precedence pinned by evaluate().

reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the needs-regroom state machine (livespec_orchestrator_beads_fabro.regroom): the three Scenario-9 transitions are exercised through the real store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient (the hermetic CI backend). The bound node id is the exit-by-filing-ready-slices journey; the entry-via-DoR-failure and entry-via-non-convergence-bounce transitions are sibling tests in the same module.

heading: ## Scenario 10 — Dispatcher holds a manual-admission spec-change item

clauses.scenario: Scenario 10 — Dispatcher holds a manual-admission spec-change item

reason: Integration-tier acceptance for Scenario 10's realization under the work-item-state-machine lifecycle (epic bd-ib-vvrxcb, slice bd-ib-dnw2ei, gap-dpk6g22t): the prior human-gated text marker is replaced by the first-class admission_policy field, so a spec-change / risky item is HELD at the admission valve as admission_policy=manual and surfaced for the maintainer, never launched into a fabro sandbox. The dispatch CLI is driven through the real store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient (the hermetic CI backend) with run_dispatch recorded; the bound node id is the manual-hold journey (item stays ready, failed at admission-held); the journal and admits-auto-item cases are sibling tests in the same module.

heading: ## Scenario 11 — Dispatcher bounces a non-converging slice to backlog

clauses.scenario: Scenario 11 — Dispatcher bounces a non-converging slice to backlog

reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the Dispatcher's non-convergence bounce (slice bd-ib-dnw2ei, gap-rs4tkntz): under the work-item-state-machine lifecycle the bounce target is the first-class backlog status (the slice leaves the WIP for re-grooming), not the prior needs-regroom label. The dispatch CLI is driven through the real store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient (the hermetic CI backend); the bound node id is the stalled-slice bounce-to-backlog journey; the DOT-marker bounce, the no-bounce-on-ordinary-failure/green cases, and the predicate cases are sibling tests in the same module.

heading: ## Scenario 12 — Dispatcher emits calibration telemetry
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the Dispatcher's calibration telemetry (work-item livespec-impl-beads-yfsv4j, gap-ajq7ynr4): the dispatch CLI is driven through the real store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient (the hermetic CI backend) to a terminal outcome, then the EXISTING on-disk Dispatcher journal (tmp/fabro-dispatch-journal.jsonl) is read back, proving a single `calibration` stage record carries the full outcome signal plus the mechanical size proxies. The bound node id is the outcome-and-size-proxies journey; the unobservable-proxy-is-null (not zero) case, the rides-the-existing-journal-with-no-new-service case, the non-convergence bounced_to_regroom case, and the journal-record-shape pin are sibling tests in the same module.

heading: ## Scenario 13 — Calibration analysis pass proposes advisory thresholds

clauses.scenario: Scenario 13 — Calibration analysis pass proposes advisory thresholds

reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the periodic calibration analysis pass (livespec_orchestrator_beads_fabro.calibration_analysis): a real on-disk Dispatcher journal seeded with behavior 6's flat calibration-stage records is read back through the production seam and analyze_calibration proposes ceiling thresholds. The bound node id is the advisory-and-never-auto-enforced guardrail (the proposal flags advisory/not-adopted and mutates no journal state); the correlation-proposes-a-ceiling Then, the not-an-always-on-service Then, the decline-on-no-signal cases, and the no-false-zero proxy handling are sibling tests in the same module.

heading: ## Scenario 14 — Fabro non-convergence routes back to the Dispatcher

clauses.scenario: Scenario 14 — Fabro non-convergence routes back to the Dispatcher

reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the single Fabro workflow-DOT tweak (work-item livespec-impl-beads-rw75ym, gap-f7kcvboh): the shipped .fabro/workflows/implement-work-item/workflow.fabro is read and asserted to carry the fix-loop cap (the existing janitor node-visit governor) plus a non-converged exit edge (janitor Exhausted -> the non_converged terminal command node, NOT the human gate) that routes back to the Dispatcher, within Fabro's existing DOT vocabulary and requiring no platform/setup change. The bound node id is the exit-edge-routing assertion; the cap, terminal-node, and shared-sentinel cases are sibling tests in the same module.

heading: ## Closed-item integrity
reason: Added by the closed-item-integrity revise pass (v007). The shared heading_coverage check is satisfied by enumerating every SPECIFICATION/ H2 heading; the closed-item-integrity invariant's mechanical guard (the closed_item_integrity check) is not yet built, so this constraint heading binds to its real test via the governed propose-change/revise resulting_files[] loop once the gap-tied impl work-item that wires the check lands its exercising test. The clauses[] gap-id->scenario link is deferred to that implementation step (impl-beads ships no clauses[] entries or livespec_spec_clauses extractor yet; adopting the clauses[] map is itself the first implementation work-item).

reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the closed_item_integrity dev-tooling check (work-items livespec-impl-beads-fxt/xe0/oaq; gap-j2femmn7 invariant, gap-gtamowt3 + gap-3wbdenoy check): the check's main() is driven through the real store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient (the hermetic CI backend) with a fixture tests/heading-coverage.json carrying the clauses[] gap-id->scenario map. The bound node id is the case-1 finding journey (a TODO-bound closed gap-tied item surfaces a closed-item-integrity warning at exit 0 in the default warn mode); the fail-mode exit-non-zero, the missing-resolution:completed-label, the unresolvable-gap-id, and the case-2 fully-proven-no-finding cases are sibling tests in the same module. Bound at the gap-tied impl step before its own resolution:completed closure (the check is its own first subject).

heading: ## Scenario 17 — orchestrate operator-surface defaults
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the three orchestrate operator-surface defaults, driven through the orchestrate CLI's public main() entry point: bare orchestrate runs the read-only plan walkthrough without erroring on the missing subcommand (the bound node id; siblings cover the no-SystemExit(2) assertion explicitly); an omitted --repo resolves to the current working directory while an explicit --repo overrides and an unresolvable path is a precondition error exit 3; and console output is human-readable Markdown by default while --json renders the same payload as JSON. The spec-side and impl-side next surfaces are driven through the CLI's public runner seam so the acceptance is hermetic, and the defaults are a property of the CLI regardless of backend.

heading: ## Worker credential projection
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the worker credential-projection contract (work-items bd-ib-di442r, bd-ib-3ti4jf, bd-ib-g7e34u): the bound node id drives the Dispatcher overlay materialization seam with injected host credential, clock, and fleet manifest, then proves the run-scoped mode-600 overlay carries both the existing Claude OAuth env and the Codex auth.json file projection materialized through a prepare step. Sibling tests in tests/integration/test_worker_credential_projection_scenarios18_19.py and tests/livespec_orchestrator_beads_fabro/commands/test_dispatcher_dual_cred.py cover stale-credential refusal, direct host auth.json read, non-rotatable snapshot projection, and Codex implementer adapter selection. The live dual-provider dispatch proof remains a provisioned-environment acceptance check because this host lacks the required Fabro LLM provider and sandbox provisioning.

heading: ## Scenario 18 — Dispatcher projects a non-rotatable subscription credential into a worker sandbox
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for Scenario 18: the bound node id exercises the Dispatcher materialization seam and verifies a fresh host Codex credential is transformed into a non-rotatable snapshot, carried in the overlay, and written by a sandbox prepare step to `$CODEX_HOME/auth.json` while the Claude OAuth env remains present in the same overlay. Lower-level sibling tests prove the refresh token is replaced with the inert sentinel and never leaks the host refresh token. The scenario's worker-authenticates clause still needs the first live dual-provider dispatch proof in a Fabro-provisioned environment.

heading: ## Scenario 19 — Dispatcher refuses dispatch when the credential freshness gate fails
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for Scenario 19: the bound node id drives the Dispatcher materialization seam with an injected stale host credential and proves dispatch refuses before writing the run-scoped overlay, surfacing the `codex login` renewal message instead of projecting a credential that cannot outlive the run budget. Sibling tests cover the missing-credential refusal and the pure freshness-gate boundary.

heading: ## Scenario 20 — Review gate routes a green build through advisory code review before PR
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for Scenario 20: the bound node id inspects the shipped implement-work-item Fabro graph and verifies the implementer nodes (`implement`, `fix`, `pr`, and `review_fix`) use the Codex-routable `acp_adapter` input while the review-only node uses the separate Claude `review_adapter`; sibling tests prove the review adapter is pinned through ANTHROPIC_MODEL + CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL and no ACP node hard-codes the Claude adapter. The graph's approve/fix/ship-on-cap edge behavior is codified in the workflow DOT and remains subject to the first live dual-provider dispatch acceptance check.

heading: ## Scenario 21 — Codex skills picker discovers orchestrate by short name
reason: Top-of-pyramid live Codex TUI acceptance for the operator discovery path: starts the real Codex TUI, opens /skills, chooses List skills, searches the short name orchestrate, and requires the rendered Skill row to include the livespec-orchestrator-beads-fabro plugin context.

heading: ## Dispatcher admission, WIP cap, and post-merge acceptance
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the two human-delegable valves + the per-repo WIP cap (epic bd-ib-vvrxcb, slice bd-ib-dnw2ei). The bound node id drives the loop admission valve (admit highest-rank ready items up to wip_cap, set assignee, ready -> active) through the real store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient; the manual-admission hold (Scenario 23), the complete-merges-on-green-into-acceptance (Scenario 24), and the accept-per-acceptance_policy + reject routing (Scenario 25) are bound by their own scenario headings in the same module.

heading: ## Scenario 22 — Dispatcher admits the top-ranked ready item up to the per-repo WIP cap
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the admission valve + per-repo WIP cap (slice bd-ib-dnw2ei): with wip_cap=2 and three admission-eligible ready items ranked a0/a1/a2, the loop admits a0 and a1 (sets an assignee, transitions each to active) and leaves a2 at ready (capacity-deferred). Driven through the real dispatcher loop CLI + store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient with run_dispatch recorded.

heading: ## Scenario 23 — Dispatcher holds a manual-admission item until approved
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the manual-admission hold (slice bd-ib-dnw2ei): a ready item whose effective admission_policy is manual is not transitioned to active and is surfaced for the maintainer to approve. Driven through the real dispatcher loop CLI + store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient with run_dispatch recorded (never launched).

heading: ## Scenario 24 — complete merges on green into the acceptance state
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for complete merges-on-green into acceptance (slice bd-ib-dnw2ei): a green dispatch transitions the active item to the observable acceptance state (merged + live) rather than straight to done. Driven through the real dispatch CLI + store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient; the journal shows ledger-complete + acceptance-ai-pass and no ledger-accept under the default ai-then-human policy.

heading: ## Scenario 25 — accept confirms post-ship per acceptance_policy
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for accept-per-acceptance_policy (slice bd-ib-dnw2ei): ai-then-human parks the merged item in acceptance until a human confirms; the bound node id is the ai-then-human park journey. The ai-only-confirms-to-done case and the reject routing (rework -> active; re-groom -> backlog, exercised through the real store seam + the pure reject_routing) are sibling tests in the same module.

heading: ## Scenario 31 — orchestrate human valve actions
